It is classified within the family Bombyliidae, which is a large and diverse group of flies that are often mistaken for bees due to their similar appearance. The family Bombyliidae is further divided into several subfamilies and genera, with Pseudoperistylus microceras being one of the many species within this group. The classification of Pseudoperistylus microceras is based on its physical characteristics, such as its body shape, color, and other distinctive features. The classification of Pseudoperistylus microceras is an ongoing process in the field of entomology, with new discoveries and research continually refining our understanding of this species and its place within the larger family Bombyliidae.
